description: Execute the dual-agent pipeline to generate Robot Framework test scripts from an API document.
prerequisites: An API specification document (OpenAPI JSON/YAML, Markdown, or plain text) must be provided or referenced.

steps.description: Read the pipeline configuration and agent definitions from .agents/SOP.json
Read all reference files to learn the project's test style
Read the API document provided by the user (file path or pasted content)
Extract every endpoint, method, parameter, header, and response code from the API document — nothing else
Derive happy-path and failure scenarios ONLY from what the document explicitly states
Write keyword names as plain English sentences — no variables, no technical jargon
Generate the .robot file with ZERO variables in test cases — keyword calls only
Generate the .resource file with full keyword implementations and # Source: citation comments
Generate a JSON summary of endpoint coverage
Re-read the original API document and all generated files from Phase 1
Systematically verify all 9 checklist items (V1–V9) from SOP.json
checklist: V1: Every endpoint has at least one test case
V2: The .robot file has ZERO ${...} variables
V3: Every keyword has a # Source: comment
V4: No hallucinated endpoints, params, or status codes
V6: Keywords read as plain English sentences
V8: State passed via Set Test Variable, not returns in .robot

steps.description: Score each category (factual_accuracy, readability, completeness, style_match) out of 100 and set verdict to APPROVED or REVISION_REQUIRED

conditions.if_revision_required: Return structured feedback with issues array and repeat Phase 1 from step 4 (max 2 retries)
conditions.if_approved: Notify the user that the files are ready for review
conditions.if_retries_exhausted: Escalate to human with the full issues list
